description: On either side of the face, 'meshoi' is the soft region below the eye and between the nose, mouth, and ear. A cheek may redden, move, or receive a touch; the noun leaves the meaning of that change to the sentence.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the sound through the nose, and release into middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region for unvoiced 'sh', then round the lips around stressed middle-height back 'o'. Raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i' for the separate final syllable.

semantic_domains.physical: the soft region on either side of the face
